111 Appellant, K’moi Corraspe, seeks reversal ofhis convictions in the Superior Court ofthe
Virgin Islands ( Superior Court ) for first degree murder and possession of a firearm during a
crime ofviolence V I CODEANN tit 14, §§ 922(a)(1), 2253(a) We first consider his argument
that his conviction for possession of a firearm during a crime of violence should be reversed
because there was insufficient evidence to prove beyond a reasonable doubt that he possessed a
firearm as defined by subsection 451(1) oftitle 23
112 He also seeks to have his convictions vacated and a new trial granted, arguing that (1) his
Due Process rights, as articulated in Brady v Maryland, 373 U S 83 (1963),' were violated by the
late disclosure ofevidence five days before trial, (2) his Sixth Amendment right ofconfrontation
was violated when Detective Nigel James relayed hearsay identifications of Corraspe as the
shooter by two non testifying witnesses and when Dr Francisco Landron testified to the victim 3
cause ofdeath based on information he had not personally obtained, (3) his Due Process right to a
fairtrial was violated through multiple instances ofprosecutorial misconductwhen the prosecution
elicited the testimony of Detective James, referenced extra record evidence of witness
intimidation, and emphasized the testimony of Detective James, (4) his Fifth Amendment Due
Process right to counsel and a fair trial and his Sixth Amendment right to be present and have
counsel at every critical stage of the prosecution were violated when, during deliberations, the
judge, without consulting defense counsel, refused the jury 3 request for Valencia Charles’
testimony to be read, and (5) his Due Process right to a fair trial was violated because the
cumulative effectofthe foregoing errors and thepaucityofevidence identifying himasthe shooter

[ BACKGROUND
113 On December IO, 2015, OfficerJermaine Carty was dispatched to Contant Knolls afterthe
Virgin Islands Police Department ( VIPD ) received a report of gunshots in that area It was
reported that there was a victim and that the assailant fled in the direction of “the Old Mill, a
nearby business establishment Carty confirmed that Contant Knolls is a government housing
community located on St Thomas and is considered a high crime area As the officer described
the scene, there are apartmentbuildings to the leftand rightwith thecommunity centerin between
Carty arrived at the scene at approximately 1 l 50 p m where he saw a black man lying face up in
a pool ofblood, dressed in a blood soaked white t shirt and dark pants There were two people by
the victim applying pressure on a wound in the uppershoulder/neck area Carty had approached
the body and saw that the white shirt was “full of blood” due to a wound to the victim s
shoulder/neck
114 Eight months later, on July 8, 2016, the People filed an information in the Superior Court
On September 28, 2016, the same day that Corraspe received his advice ofrights, the People filed
an arrest warrant and arrest report ’ Ultimately, Corraspe was tried on an amended information3
and convicted ofcount one, first degree murder, 14 V I C § 922(a)(l), and count two, possession

1l8 Afterconsideringothermotions notrelevant to this appeal,juryselection began The court
voirdired thejury venirewith both general questions and case specific questions designed to elicit

1132 The territorial Medical Examiner, Dr Landron, was the nextwitness forthe People In this
capacity, it is his duty to determine the cause and manner ofdeath, the time ofdeath, the identity
of the deceased, collect evidence from the body, and provide expert witness testimony in court
The ‘ cause ofdeath” is the injury or disease that leads to the person’s death “Manner ofdeath ’
is the medical examiner’s opinion based on the surrounding circumstances ofthe death, such as
toxicology, etc There are five manners of death, natural, suicide, accident, homicide, and
undetermined In orderto make these determinations, the medical examiner conducts an autopsy
After conducting an autopsy, the medical examiner creates a death certificate and an autopsy
report, which are kept for future reference
1133 Dr Landron further explained that, ifa forensic pathologist reviews the notes and autopsy
report ofan autopsy done by someone else and any relevant photographs ofthe death, they are
conducting a “peer review in order to offer an opinion as to cause and manner ofdeath Based
on his qualifications, Dr Landron was qualified as an expert in forensic pathology
